entity.summary: Russia-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the United States in 2022 and later forced to suspend services in March 2025 after EU sanctions, Tether wallet blocking, and international law-enforcement action against its web infrastructure.
entity.notes: Death date follows the March 2025 service suspension and seizure period. A later Grinex-related customer-base transition is not modeled here as a clean successor because public claims about continuity are disputed.

events.title: Garantex began operating
events.description: Garantex operated as a Russia-linked centralized cryptocurrency exchange from 2019.
events.notes: Month-level launch date based on public background references.

events.title: U.S. Treasury sanctioned Garantex
events.description: The U.S. Treasury sanctioned Garantex as part of an action against Russia-based Hydra Market and a ransomware-enabling virtual currency exchange.
events.notes: Regulatory action predating the 2025 service collapse.

events.title: Services suspended after sanctions and wallet blocking
events.description: Garantex suspended services after Tether blocked wallets on the platform, with international enforcement activity and web-infrastructure seizure following in the same period.
events.notes: Used as death_date because the platform stopped providing services during the sanctions and seizure period.

evidence.title: Garantex background reference
evidence.notes: Used only for launch-month and background cross-checking.

evidence.title: Treasury sanctions Russia-based Hydra and ransomware-enabling virtual currency exchange Garantex
evidence.notes: Primary U.S. sanctions source for Garantex.

evidence.title: Sanctioned Russian crypto exchange suspends services as Tether blocks wallets
evidence.notes: Used for the March 2025 service suspension and wallet-blocking event.

evidence.title: U.S. seizes Russian crypto exchange popular with cybercriminals
evidence.notes: Used for web-infrastructure seizure and indictment context.
